seed: spark vs ash; hunger_vs_restraint; diagonal_argument; wave_vertical; radial against split_screen; jagged_edges; memory_vs_erasure; star; resilience_fragility | abstract variation request 276; target batch count 500
title: Erosion of Resilience
reasoning: This artwork explores the duality of 'spark' and 'ash' through a muted yet radiant palette, invoking emotions of fragility and resilience. The color choices of moss green and rust suggest life struggling against decay, while the bone white introduces moments of clarity amidst chaos. The layout is dominated centrally by jagged edges of dense colors, symbolizing the struggle of memory against the erasure of past, while the periphery remains intentionally quieter, creating a contrast of pressure and breath. Patterns collide in the center, where the 'braid' of sparks emanates from the solid cross of ash, disrupted by intentional broken pixels to echo imperfections and memories that linger. The flow from bottom to top reflects the struggle towards resilience, building to a peak before gently receding, establishing a sense of motion despite the weight of struggle.

layout.name: overfilled_center
layout.description: The center is dense and saturated while the border struggles to contain it.

composition.name: memory_vs_motion
composition.idea: Faded repeated marks are interrupted by a cleaner advancing path.

referenceExample.title: Repair After Guilt
referenceExample.pattern: dark residue patched by small ivory and gold marks

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
